---
title: "Geophysical Fluid Dynamics and Atmospheric Modeling"
subtitle: "Track F — Physical Systems"
date: "2025-01-01"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
number-sections: true
---

# Geophysical Fluid Dynamics and Atmospheric Modeling

**Track**: Track F — Physical Systems

The dynamics of rotating, stratified fluids on planetary scales: the mathematical foundation for weather prediction, ocean modeling, and climate science.

## Table of Contents

1. Governing Equations on a Rotating Earth
2. Geostrophic Balance and Thermal Wind
3. Rossby Waves and Planetary Wave Dynamics
4. Baroclinic Instability
5. Quasi-Geostrophic Theory
6. Parameterization of Sub-Grid Processes
7. Numerical Methods for Atmospheric Models
8. Data Assimilation for Weather Prediction
9. Climate Modeling and Earth System Science
10. Climate Model Emulation and SciML in Climate Science

---

## Chapter 1: Governing Equations on a Rotating Earth

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 2: Geostrophic Balance and Thermal Wind

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 3: Rossby Waves and Planetary Wave Dynamics

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 4: Baroclinic Instability

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 5: Quasi-Geostrophic Theory

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 6: Parameterization of Sub-Grid Processes

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 7: Numerical Methods for Atmospheric Models

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 8: Data Assimilation for Weather Prediction

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 9: Climate Modeling and Earth System Science

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 10: Climate Model Emulation and SciML in Climate Science

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*
